200.description: Service is live.

On success: a 15-min access token + the httpOnly refresh cookie (status `authenticated`). For an operator with opt-in MFA: status `mfa_required` with NO tokens, plus the short-lived httpOnly `ftd_mfa` challenge cookie — complete it at POST /auth/mfa/verify.
Reads the refresh token from the httpOnly cookie, rotates it, and re-sets the cookie; returns a new access token.
Current principal + effective permissions.
Verify a TOTP code for the in-progress login challenge. On success the session is issued (ftd_refresh set, ftd_mfa cleared); on failure the per-challenge attempt counter advances.
Redeem a one-time backup code for the in-progress login challenge and issue the session. Remember-device is not offered on the recovery path.
Begin opt-in MFA enrolment (password re-auth). Returns the otpauth URI + QR to import into an authenticator app. MFA is NOT active until /setup/confirm.
Confirm enrolment with the first code: activates MFA and returns the one-time backup codes (shown ONCE).

204.description: Disable MFA (password + a current TOTP or backup code). Clears MFA state and revokes remembered devices.

200.description: Replace the backup codes (password + a current TOTP or backup code); returns the new set ONCE.

204.description: Administrator-only: reset a target operator’s MFA and revoke their sessions, devices, and live challenges.

200.description: List the operator’s active trusted ("remember this device") devices — empty when the feature is off.

204.description: Revoke one of the operator’s own trusted devices (the next sign-in from it re-prompts for MFA).

202.description: Start a self-service password reset. ALWAYS responds 202 { status: "reset_initiated" } (no user enumeration). Sets the httpOnly ftd_pwreset challenge cookie ONLY when the account exists AND has confirmed MFA — MFA-enabled state is never revealed.

200.description: Step 2: verify the second factor (6-digit TOTP or one-time backup code) ONCE and stamp the challenge (factor_verified_at). On success 200 { status: "code_verified" } — the TOTP replay floor is advanced / the backup code consumed, and the later /verify needs no code. IDEMPOTENT for an already-stamped challenge (a Back/retry does not re-spend the factor). On a bad/expired factor the per-challenge attempt counter advances and 401 Auth.ResetInvalidCode is returned. The verify-code call sets NO cookie and issues NO token.

Step 3: set the new password. Requires a prior factor stamp from verify-code (else 401 Auth.ResetFactorRequired); takes the new password ONLY (no code). On success the password is changed, ALL sessions/devices/challenges are revoked, the ftd_pwreset cookie is cleared, and NO session is issued (a fresh /login is required). The bound IP/UA fingerprint is re-enforced on this call too.

204.description: Administrator-only: set a working password for a target operator who cannot self-serve the MFA-gated reset, and revoke their sessions, devices, and live challenges. No session/token is issued. Self-reset via this path is forbidden (use the self-service reset).

202.description: Ask an administrator to reset the password for an account. ALWAYS responds 202 { status: "reset_request_received" } and ALWAYS sets the httpOnly ftd_pwreq cookie (a decoy on non-created branches) — no user enumeration: unknown email, duplicate open request, and cooldown are indistinguishable from a fresh request. At most one open request per account; poll the status endpoint (with the cookie) for the owner-only outcome.

200.description: Poll the caller's own reset request, keyed by the httpOnly ftd_pwreq cookie. NEVER 401/404 — a missing/unknown/decoy token reads as { status: 'pending' }. On an approved, unclaimed request the call CLAIMS it: a standard ftd_pwreset challenge cookie (factor pre-stamped 'admin_approval', bound to this call's IP/UA) is set so POST /auth/password/reset/verify can finish with the new password. Re-polling re-mints (latest wins); after completion the status stays 'approved' with no cookie. POST because the claim is a side effect; SameSite=Strict is the CSRF control.
Administrator-only: the reset-request queue, PENDING first, newest first (max 100). Account emails are ALWAYS masked.
Administrator-only: one reset request incl. coarse device metadata (ipPrefix — never a raw IP — plus a User-Agent summary and the raw UA). 404 Auth.ResetRequestNotFound when unknown.

Approve a pending reset request (verify the requester's identity OUT-OF-BAND first). The requester's polling browser then claims a factor-pre-stamped set-password challenge — the admin never chooses or sees a password. Returns the refreshed detail. Errors: 404 Auth.ResetRequestNotFound · 409 Auth.ResetRequestAlreadyDecided · 409 Auth.ResetRequestExpired · 403 Auth.SelfResetForbidden.
Deny a pending reset request; the requester is notified (durable receipt). Same contract and error set as approve.

parameters.name: filter[read]
parameters.description: true = read only, false = unread only.

200.description: Recipient-scoped paged notifications + the recipient's total unreadCount.
Mark ONE of the caller's own notifications read (404 if not owned/known). Idempotent.
Mark ALL of the caller's unread notifications read; returns unreadCount = 0.

204.description: Sets a short-lived (60s) httpOnly cookie (`ftd_stream`, scope `stream:read`) used to authenticate GET /dashboard/stream. No body — the credential is never exposed to JS.

200.description: Active ISO-4217 currency catalog.
Authenticated operator profile/settings.
Update authenticated operator profile/settings.
Authenticated operator notification preferences.
Update authenticated operator notification preferences.
List roles with their permission codes.
The permission catalog.

Admin-only paged user list for the password-reset operator picker + status panel: id, displayName, status, roles, a server-side MASKED email, and lockout telemetry (locked / failedLoginCount / lastLoginAt). No raw PII.

201.description: Grant a permission to a role (self-escalation guarded).
Assign a role to a user (self-escalation guarded).
Persist a rule-based screening decision with its safety label.

200.description: Screen an address through the bound risk provider without persisting a decision.
Screening history, newest first; each carries its isSimulated flag.
The customer's transactions over the date range.
Top-line KPIs over all customers.
KYC status distribution (zero-filled).
Most-recently-updated customer (PII masked); data is null when none exist.
The most-recently-updated customers (PII masked), newest first.
Daily rollup series for the selected metric.

parameters.name: reveal
parameters.description: true → return UNMASKED PII. Honored ONLY for holders of `customers.pii.reveal`; otherwise silently masked (default-deny). Every effective reveal is audited.

parameters.name: filter[active]
parameters.description: true = ACTIVE, false = not-ACTIVE (used when filter[status] is absent).

200.description: Masked by default; unmasked only with customers.pii.reveal + ?reveal=true.

201.description: Creates a customer + default wallet; returns masked detail.

parameters.name: reveal
parameters.description: true → return UNMASKED PII. Honored ONLY for holders of `customers.pii.reveal`; otherwise silently masked (default-deny). Every effective reveal is audited.

200.description: Masked by default; unmasked only with customers.pii.reveal + ?reveal=true.
Updates a customer (rowVersion-guarded); returns masked detail. Administrator-only (customers.update).
KYC verification history, newest first.
Data-minimized KYC credential preview.
The customer's default wallet (balance + limits).
Updates the customer's wallet limits (rowVersion-guarded).

info.title: Fintech Dashboard API
info.description: System-of-record backend (NestJS on Fastify + Prisma + PostgreSQL).

mfaEnabled.description: Whether this operator has opt-in MFA enabled (drives the Settings MFA card).

lastLoginAt.description: ISO timestamp of the most recent successful sign-in (stamped at login), or null before the first one. Drives the Settings "last sign-in" readout.

status.description: Login completed — a session was issued.

code.description: The 6-digit code from the authenticator app.

rememberDevice.description: Trust this device and skip MFA until expiry — honoured only when remember-device is enabled.

code.description: A one-time backup recovery code (case-insensitive; the hyphen is optional).

password.description: The operator’s current password — re-auth for this sensitive change.

otpauthUri.description: otpauth:// URI to import into an authenticator app.

qrDataUrl.description: PNG data-URL of the QR encoding the otpauth URI.

code.description: The first 6-digit code from the authenticator app, proving enrolment.

backupCodes.description: One-time backup recovery codes — shown ONCE; store them now.

code.description: A current 6-digit TOTP code OR a one-time backup code (XXXXX-XXXXX).

userId.description: The target operator whose MFA enrolment is to be reset.

ipPrefix.description: Coarse network prefix bound at issue — NOT a full IP.

email.description: The account email to start a reset for.

status.description: Constant acknowledgement — returned for every email (no enumeration).

code.description: A current 6-digit TOTP code OR a one-time backup code (XXXXX-XXXXX).

status.description: the second factor was verified and the challenge stamped. Proceed to set the new password via /verify. NO tokens are issued.

newPassword.description: The new password (min 12 chars; must include upper, lower, digit, and symbol).

status.description: The password was changed and all sessions revoked. NO tokens are issued — sign in again.

targetUserId.description: The target operator whose password is to be reset.

newPassword.description: The new password (min 12 chars; must include upper, lower, digit, and symbol).

email.description: The account email to request an administrator password reset for.

status.description: Constant acknowledgement — returned for EVERY create call (new, duplicate, cooldown, or unknown email alike; no enumeration).

The caller's own request state, keyed by the httpOnly ftd_pwreq cookie. NEVER 401/404 — a missing/unknown/decoy token reads as 'pending' (indistinguishable). On 'approved' (not yet completed) the response ALSO sets the ftd_pwreset challenge cookie so the standard /auth/password/reset/verify step can finish the reset.

emailMasked.description: The requesting account email — ALWAYS masked.

expiresAt.description: When the request lapses (covers pending AND approved-unclaimed).

decidedByName.description: Display name of the deciding administrator (masked email fallback).

completedAt.description: Stamped when the granted set-password challenge was consumed.

expiresAt.description: When the request lapses (covers pending AND approved-unclaimed).

decidedByName.description: Display name of the deciding administrator (masked email fallback).

completedAt.description: Stamped when the granted set-password challenge was consumed.

ipPrefix.description: Coarse network prefix bound at request time — the raw IP is never stored or shown.

deviceSummary.description: Coarse device summary parsed from the stored User-Agent at read time.

userAgent.description: The raw User-Agent as presented (service-truncated to 400 chars).

params.description: Allowlisted, non-PII interpolation values for the FE; null when none.

unreadCount.description: Recipient's TOTAL unread count (independent of page/filter).
Recipient's unread count AFTER the operation.

jobTitle.description: Operator-entered title stored as settings state.

id.description: User id — the value used as targetUserId by admin tools.

displayName.description: Operator display name (the picker label); null if unset.

emailMasked.description: Email MASKED server-side (raw address never leaves the service). For the operator picker.

locked.description: True when the account is locked (status LOCKED or a future lockedUntil). Drives the "Kilitli" badge.

lastLoginAt.description: Last successful sign-in (ISO 8601); null if never. Rendered relative on the client.

isSimulated.description: Safety flag — must be true while the rule-based engine is bound.
True for rule-based screening (never a live vendor regulatory decision).
True for rule-based screening (never a live vendor regulatory decision).

originalTransactionId.description: Required for REVERSAL — the POSTED transaction to reverse (amountMinor + currency must match it).

currency.description: ISO-4217 code; both legs share one currency (no cross-currency).

amountMinor.description: Signed net for the customer (minor units), as a decimal string: CREDIT > 0, DEBIT < 0.

avg.description: Whole years; null when no dated births.

balanceMinor.description: Integer minor units, as a decimal string.

value.description: Numeric value serialized as a string to preserve bigint/numeric precision for counts and minor-unit volume.

items.description: One row per rolled-up day. Currency-dimensioned volume rows are summed per day for this read model.

fullName.description: Masked by default (e.g. "Ada L***"); raw only with `customers.pii.reveal` + `?reveal=true`.

email.description: Masked by default (e.g. "a***@e***.com"); raw only with `customers.pii.reveal` + `?reveal=true`.

phone.description: Masked last 4 by default (e.g. "*** *** 1234"); raw only with `customers.pii.reveal` + `?reveal=true`.

walletNumber.description: Masked last 4 by default (e.g. "************3456"); raw only with `customers.pii.reveal` + `?reveal=true`.

nationalIdLast4.description: Last 4 of the national ID in ALL modes; the full national ID is NEVER served (no decrypt path).

country.description: Always raw (low-identifying, retained for ops UX).

city.description: Masked to `null` by default; raw only with `customers.pii.reveal` + `?reveal=true`.

postalCode.description: Masked to `null` by default; raw only with `customers.pii.reveal` + `?reveal=true`.

line1.description: Masked to first-char + `***` by default; raw only with `customers.pii.reveal` + `?reveal=true`.

fullName.description: Masked by default (e.g. "Ada L***"); raw only with `customers.pii.reveal` + `?reveal=true`.

email.description: Masked by default (e.g. "a***@e***.com"); raw only with `customers.pii.reveal` + `?reveal=true`.

phone.description: Masked last 4 by default (e.g. "*** *** 1234"); raw only with `customers.pii.reveal` + `?reveal=true`.

walletNumber.description: Masked last 4 by default (e.g. "************3456"); raw only with `customers.pii.reveal` + `?reveal=true`.

nationalIdLast4.description: Last 4 of the national ID in ALL modes; the full national ID is NEVER served (no decrypt path).

contractSigned.description: Commercial flag — NOT a KYC state.

id.description: Pairwise DID-like preview id scoped to this customer.

kycVerified.description: Whether the customer is currently KYC verified.

phone.description: Digits (7-15), optional leading +; separators (spaces/dashes/parentheses) are normalized away.

nationalId.description: Turkish national ID (TC Kimlik No). Stored column-encrypted; only the last 4 are ever returned.

phone.description: Digits (7-15), optional leading +; separators (spaces/dashes/parentheses) are normalized away.

contractSigned.description: Commercial flag — NOT a KYC state.

rowVersion.description: Optimistic-concurrency token from the last read; mismatch → 409.

balanceMinor.description: Balance in integer minor units, as a decimal string.

availableBalanceMinor.description: Available balance (balance − holds), minor units, as a decimal string.

dailyLimitMinor.description: Daily limit, minor units, as a decimal string.

monthlyLimitMinor.description: Monthly limit, minor units, as a decimal string.

dailyLimit.description: Daily limit in MAJOR units (must be < monthlyLimit).

monthlyLimit.description: Monthly limit in MAJOR units (must be > dailyLimit).

rowVersion.description: Optimistic-concurrency token from the last read; mismatch → 409.
